page: OVHcloud c2-7 is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Haswell processor running at 3.0 GHz. The CPU configuration does not use hyperthreading, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 7.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes 50 GB of local SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the lower tiers for compute and memory throughput. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng benchmarks score 864.27 and 1718.26 respectively, placing them in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 175.64 GB/sec, which drops to 20.03 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The Passmark CPU score is 2735.63, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 1757.0, both falling within the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

For specific workloads, the instance achieves 2.71 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 2.42 GB/sec. Large language model inference using a small 135M model reaches 212.61 tokens per second for prompt processing and an average-tier 74.86 tokens per second for text generation. This hardware profile is suited for lightweight dedicated compute tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale database operations where dedicated CPU allocation is required but overall resource demands are modest.
description: OVHcloud c2-7 is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel Haswell architecture running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 7.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.5 GB of memory per core, and includes 50 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results place its overall compute and memory performance in the lower percentiles, with a Passmark CPU score of 2735.63 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1757.0. Despite these lower-tier scores, the instance maintains stable performance for lightweight workloads, achieving 2.71 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 74.86 tokens per second for small LLM text generation. This instance is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight database operations, and entry-level dedicated compute tasks where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over shared resources.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-7 is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 2 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage, designed for entry-level dedicated compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual server with local SSD storage, designed for lightweight compute tasks and basic web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated Intel Haswell vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz without hyperthreading
Equipped with 7.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local SSD storage
Delivers 2.71 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Processes small 135M LLM text generation at an average of 74.86 tokens per second
Best suited for lightweight dedicated compute, basic web serving, and small database tasks
